title: AI Named the 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner (Shocking Prediction)
summary: A YouTube video demonstrates AI Master, a platform that runs Claude Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.5 Flash side-by-side on ten 2026 FIFA World Cup prediction tasks. The creator uses real-time predictions to be "graded" on a future outcome date, positioning this as a live stress test of model behavior and trustworthiness across prediction types rather than a traditional benchmark.
writeup: The video runs a platform called AI Master that puts Claude Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.5 Flash side-by-side on ten 2026 FIFA World Cup prediction tasks. Rather than a static benchmark, the setup frames the exercise as a live stress test: real-time predictions are logged to be graded against a future outcome date, comparing how the models behave across different prediction types. The stated aim is to probe model behavior and trustworthiness under conditions where the answers cannot be known in advance.
